name: Philodendron 'Caramel Marble'

morphology.leaf_shape: Deeply serrated to pinnately lobed margins with saw-tooth edges, lanceolate to narrow-oblong blade shape
morphology.leaf_ratio: Elongated leaves, approximately 4:1 to 6:1 length-to-width ratio (leaves 30–60 cm long × 8–15 cm wide)
morphology.texture: Thick, leathery (coriaceous), stiff, with a glossy waxy sheen on the adaxial surface
morphology.petiole: Long, sturdy, cylindrical, colored deep reddish-brown to dark green, slightly winged at the base
morphology.growth_habit: Slow-growing, self-heading when young, transitioning to a slow climbing/scandent habit with age, producing thick aerial roots
morphology.mature_size: Overall leaf length 30–60 cm; petioles 25–40 cm; mature height up to 1.2 meters when supported

critical_traits: Deeply serrated or pinnately lobed leaf margins, resembling a jagged saw-tooth edge
Highly variegated foliage featuring marbled, sectoral, and speckled patterns of caramel, orange, bronze, yellow, cream, and forest green
Thick, leathery, coriaceous leaf texture that is stiff to the touch
Glossy adaxial leaf finish reflecting studio light
Long, sturdy reddish-brown or dark maroon petioles
Elongated narrow-oblong to lanceolate leaf blade shape
negative_traits: Entire leaf margins without serrations or lobes
Solid green leaves with no variegation
Fenestrations or holes in the leaf blades
Thin, papery, or membranous leaf texture
Perfectly symmetrical or simple cordate leaf shapes
Winged petioles that wrap around the stem like a sheath
Soft, velvety, or pubescent leaf surfaces
habitat: Cultivated mutation originally selected from Philodendron hybridization. Thrives in high humidity (60–85%), warm temperatures (18–28°C), and bright indirect light to preserve and stimulate the colorful variegation patterns.
origin: Cultivated selection/hybrid mutation, widely propagated in specialized collections globally.
